sections_cited: Section 25 of the Sindh Arms Act

chunks.text: Page 1 of 2 ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. Jail Appeal No.S-121 of 2023. Cr. Jail Appeal No.S-125 of 2023. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 18.03.2026. Mr. Siraj Ahmed Bijarani, Assistant Prosecutor General for the State alongwith ASP Shakeel Baig from Central Prison, Hyderabad. ***************************************** ASP Shakeel Baig, present on behalf of the Jail Superintendent, Central Prison, Hyderabad, submits that the appellant, namely Haq Nawaz @ Captain, was convicted in Crime No.437 of 2021 under Sections 324, 337-D, 34 PPC of P.S. Kotri, and in Crime No.444 of 2021 under Section 25 of the Sindh Arms Act, 2013 of P.S. Kotri. He further submits that the appellant is presently confined solely on account of non-payment of “Arsh” amounting to Rs.14,20,401/- and compensation of Rs.30,000/-; however, the appellant has already served out the substantive sentence in both the aforementioned cases. He further submits that the aforesaid amount shall be arranged by “Qarshi Foundation”, Islamabad, on humanitarian grounds, and shall be deposited in the office of the Accountant of this Court through the Additional Registrar. Upon such deposit, the appellant shall be released forthwith, if not required in any other custody case. ASP further submits that he has obtained the consent of the appellant with regard to the instant appeals, and the appellant does not wish to proceed further with the same. In view of the above, since the appellant does not intend to pursue the instant Criminal Jail Appeals on merits, the same have become infructuous and are hereby disposed of accordingly, subject to payment of the aforesaid amounts.

ASP further submits that he has obtained the consent of the appellant with regard to the instant appeals, and the appellant does not wish to proceed further with the same. In view of the above, since the appellant does not intend to pursue the instant Criminal Jail Appeals on merits, the same have become infructuous and are hereby disposed of accordingly, subject to payment of the aforesaid amounts. Before parting with this order, the efforts made by “Qarshi Foundation”, Islamabad, as well as the Jail Authorities, are highly commendable and deserve due appreciation. Page 2 of 2 Let a copy of this order be communicated to the I.G. Prisons, Karachi, and the Jail Superintendent, Central Prison, Hyderabad. JUDGE Ali.
